message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
summary: Adding dry_run parameter to Image.save() method to test saving without writing to disk

type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py, Tests/test_image.py
summary: Adding backup/backup_suffix params to Image.save() to auto-backup existing files before overwrite

type: STATUS
files: src/PIL/Image.py
summary: Implemented dry_run in save(): added keyword-only dry_run=False param after format, and in body replaced fp-opening logic with 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO() elif open_fp: ... else: ...' plus guarded fp.close()/os.remove with 'and not dry_run'. Only touched the save() method signature and its file-opening/closing block; rest of Image.py untouched. Let me know where you're inserting backup logic so we don't collide on the same lines.

type: STATUS
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
summary: Implemented backup: added keyword-only backup=False, backup_suffix='.bak' params after format in save(). In body, right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' inside 'if open_fp:' block, added 'if backup and not created: shutil.copy2(filename, backup_filename)' before the append/open logic. Also added shutil import. Only touched save() signature + that one spot in the open_fp block; rest untouched. Since your dry_run changes replace the fp-opening logic in the same block, our diffs will need reconciling at merge time - my backup copy2 call must run before fp is opened for writing (i.e. before your dry_run/open_fp branching), so keep it as the first statement inside 'if open_fp:' after computing created.

type: STATUS
files: src/PIL/Image.py
summary: dry_run implementation finalized and patch.txt written. My change is: 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO() elif open_fp: created = not os.path.exists(filename); ...'. This means dry_run bypasses the 'created' computation entirely (created stays False), so your backup/copy2 check should go inside the elif/open_fp branch, guarded on 'not dry_run' is unnecessary since dry_run skips that branch already - just keep your backup logic as first line after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' inside the open_fp branch as planned, it will compose fine with my code since dry_run short-circuits before reaching it.
